page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled as a 2400 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across compute and memory subsystems. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng tests and strong multi-core performance. PassMark CPU and physics tests also place in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% tier, supported by strong read and write memory bandwidth.

In application-specific workloads, the server demonstrates average performance in database operations, Redis SET transactions, and static web serving. However, it excels in lightweight machine learning tasks, delivering top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models and strong text generation for medium models. This hardware profile offers high resource density and memory capacity, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. Suitable workloads include memory-optimized databases, caching layers, data processing pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 24 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and a 2400 GB local NVMe SSD. Powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 12 physical cores and a shared allocation model, this instance delivers a high 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. It is particularly efficient for memory-bound applications, showing strong results in small-to-medium LLM text generation and memory bandwidth tests. The combination of high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage makes this server well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, data processing, and lightweight inference workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server featuring 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 2400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance and strong compute capabilities.

tagline: An AMD EPYC server featuring high memory density and fast local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads and data processing.

Bundled 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory performance and single-core CPU benchmarks
Strong text generation speeds for small and medium LLM inference
